records.why: Still actively referenced by launcher, desktop entry, and docs/manifests.
records.reevaluateWhen: If launcher/docs migrate to orgs/riatzukiza/desktop and active references fall away.

records.why: Heavy active dependency in shadow-cljs paths, PM2 parity tests, and docs.
records.reevaluateWhen: If source-path/test/docs references are rewritten to orgs/riatzukiza/promethean.

records.why: Active workspace tooling fixture but no agreed long-term structured home yet.
records.reevaluateWhen: When its long-term home is chosen under packages/* or an org repo.

records.why: Active root CLI/tooling entrypoint referenced by scripts, commands, and skills.
records.reevaluateWhen: When the tooling home is settled and compatibility command paths can be preserved elsewhere.

records.why: Reads more like experimental/documented surface nested under reconstitute than a stable standalone root.
records.reevaluateWhen: Once the MCP surface is either folded into reconstitute docs or promoted into a real package/service.

records.why: Active live deploy/source root should eventually get a canonical structured home.
records.reevaluateWhen: When its placement under packages/services/orgs is decided and live deployment is repointed.

records.why: User explicitly directed that all threat-radar-related work normalize into orgs/open-hax/eta-mu-radar.
records.reevaluateWhen: When canonical source is moved or mirrored into orgs/open-hax/eta-mu-radar and runtime/deploy references are updated.
notes: Product framing should remove threat-centric language and emphasize authorship/agency/mutual self-interest.

records.why: Currently mostly bookkeeping; no strong active integration pressure justifies a forced move.
records.reevaluateWhen: If active product work resumes or the repo becomes more than a preserved absorbed root.

records.why: User explicitly directed that all threat-radar-related work, including planning bundles, consolidate into orgs/open-hax/eta-mu-radar.
records.reevaluateWhen: When the useful planning/spec content is folded into orgs/open-hax/eta-mu-radar docs/specs and the standalone bundle becomes redundant.
notes: Fold into orgs/open-hax/eta-mu-radar as planning/docs material rather than leave as a separate root bundle.
